analysis_note: Baseline sentiment analysis based on contextual intelligence. No substantive news content collected yet - source returned login wall.

parties.name: PH (Pakatan Harapan)
parties.notes: Incumbent coalition (17 seats in 2023). MB Aminuddin Harun. Facing royal crisis fallout.

parties.name: BN (Barisan Nasional)
parties.notes: 14 UMNO assemblymen withdrew support during April 2026 crisis (later reversed). Leadership confusion at grassroots.

parties.name: PN (Perikatan Nasional)
parties.notes: 5 seats in 2023. Expected inroads in rural Malay areas. PAS-Bersatu split (June 2026) may impact.

parties.name: DAP
parties.notes: PH component. Holds N27 Repah (Veerapan). Urban stronghold in Seremban area.

parties.name: PKR
parties.notes: PH component. MB Aminuddin Harun from PKR.

politicians.name: Aminuddin Harun
politicians.notes: Leading PH campaign. Royal crisis occurred during tenure.

politicians.name: Veerapan
politicians.notes: Confirmed incumbent. Border constituency with Johor.

politicians.name: Khairy Jamaluddin
politicians.notes: Home ground in Rembau. Competitive area.

politicians.name: Datuk Mubarak Dohak
politicians.notes: Removed by MB in April 2026 royal crisis.

politicians.name: Tunku Nadzaruddin
politicians.notes: Named by 4 Undangs during succession dispute.

politicians.name: Tuanku Muhriz
politicians.notes: Declared 'removed' by 4 Undangs in April 2026 crisis.

constituencies.name: Seri Menanti
constituencies.notes: Royal town. Ground zero of succession crisis. High sensitivity.

constituencies.name: Repah
constituencies.notes: DAP-held. Veerapan incumbent. Border with Johor.

constituencies.name: Rembau seats
constituencies.notes: Khairy's home ground. PN contesting strongly.

constituencies.name: Port Dickson seats
constituencies.notes: PH fortress. Anwar's constituency. Tourism/naval base.

constituencies.name: Seremban seats
constituencies.notes: PH urban stronghold. DAP-PKR dominance expected.

issues.notes: April 2026 crisis involving Undang removal, counter-declarations. Affects Malay voter sentiment in royal constituencies.
14 assemblymen withdrew support (later reversed). Zahid intervention required.
June 2026 split may weaken PN machinery.
Expected dominant issue in urban constituencies.

escalation_flags.description: Royal succession crisis affecting 4 constituencies (Seri Menanti, Pilah, Johol, Kuala Klawang area). Negative sentiment in traditional Malay heartland.
escalation_flags.recommendation: Priority intelligence collection on Malay voter sentiment in royal constituencies. Stakeholder engagement with Undang institutions.

escalation_flags.description: News collection returned login wall (sinarharian.com.my premium content). No substantive content for automated sentiment analysis.
escalation_flags.recommendation: Adjust collection strategy - target non-premium sources or implement authentication. Manual collection required until resolved.

escalation_flags.description: All 36 DUN classified as GAP. No 2023 election results, incumbent status, or candidate data collected.
escalation_flags.recommendation: Execute Week 1 Tier 1 priorities: SPR data scrape for 2023 results, incumbent tracking, candidate nomination monitoring.

recommendations: Prioritize non-premium news sources for collection (BHarian, Utusan, NST, The Star)
Manual SPR data collection for 2023 election results (all 36 DUN)
Deploy stakeholder interviews in royal constituencies (Seri Menanti, Pilah, Johol)
Monitor UMNO grassroots machinery status post-crisis
Track PAS-Bersatu split impact on PN campaign machinery
